Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Whole plant, strongest in flowers, young shoots, root branching points, injury sites
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables autonomous green bioluminescence in transgenic plants when co-expressed with the fungal caffeic-acid-cycle genes.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Codon-optimized nnluz integrated into the nuclear genome together with nnhisps, nnh3h and nncph produced visible self-sustained luminescence in Nicotiana tabacum and Nicotiana benthamiana. 2. Luminescence was visible at all developmental stages and was brightest in flowers. 3. qPCR was used to test nnluz transcript oscillation during the day.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable Agrobacterium-mediated transformation; PCR confirmation; Southern blot; luminescence imaging; qPCR
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Leaf injury; apical shoot removal; methyl jasmonate treatment; exposure to ripe banana skin (ethylene source); developmental stage
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutive transgene expression driven by 35S promoter
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious toxicity; chlorophyll/carotenoid content, flowering time and seed germination unchanged; median plant height increased by ~12%
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Reporter/output gene for the fungal bioluminescence pathway rather than a native plant nutrient-metabolism gene; light output depends on caffeic-acid availability.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Non-invasive reporting of plant metabolic and developmental dynamics

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports the caffeic acid cycle required for autonomous plant luminescence.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. nncph was one of four fungal genes assembled for stable expression in tobacco. 2. It was included to complete the fungal caffeic acid cycle enabling substrate recycling. 3. A cph-less construct was also assembled in methods, but the core luminous plants described used the four-gene set.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable transformation and luminescence phenotype of four-gene plants
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutive transgene expression driven by 35S promoter
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious growth burden reported for the four-gene system

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Fungal bioluminescence caffeic acid cycle integrated with plant phenylpropanoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream, entry from caffeic acid into fungal luciferin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms luciferin precursor from plant-derived caffeic acid
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts caffeic acid into hispidin using malonyl-CoA, CoA and ATP as cofactors/substrates in the fungal bioluminescence pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Whole plant, strongest in flowers and young active tissues
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for autonomous luminescence; pathway flux appears limited by caffeic acid supply feeding this step.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a flux-limited step through substrate supply; caffeic acid availability limits hispidin biosynthesis rather than malonyl-CoA, CoA or ATP.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable transformation; precursor infiltration assays; luminescence imaging
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Infiltration with caffeic acid, hispidin, luciferin, malonyl-CoA, CoA, ATP; methyl jasmonate; injury; ethylene exposure
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutive transgene expression driven by 35S promoter
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. nnhisps is a core enzyme of the introduced fungal caffeic acid cycle. 2. Injection of hispidin caused immediate bright luminescence, confirming hispidin as a direct downstream intermediate. 3. Caffeic acid supplementation increased luminescence whereas malonyl-CoA, CoA or ATP did not, indicating that caffeic acid supply limits nnhisps-mediated hispidin production.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious toxicity or major growth penalty in transgenic plants
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The step is mechanistically central because it links endogenous phenylpropanoid metabolite caffeic acid to fungal luciferin production.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms luciferin/immediate light-emitting substrate from hispidin
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates hispidin to form the fungal luciferin used by luciferase.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for conversion of hispidin to luciferin, enabling visible autonomous luminescence.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established as rate-limiting in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable transformation; pathway design; luminescence imaging; precursor infiltration logic
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutive transgene expression driven by 35S promoter
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. nnh3h was one of the four fungal genes sufficient for visible autonomous luminescence in tobacco. 2. The paper places nnh3h between hispidin formation and luciferase action in the caffeic acid cycle. 3. Its function enables endogenous luciferin generation without exogenous substrate.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gene included as a core enzyme of the introduced bioluminescent pathway, although not individually dissected genetically.
